content.question: To what extent does the growth in Hungarian selective-track enrollment reflect genuine pedagogical demand (student/family perception of learning quality gain) versus signaling/credentialing dynamics (tracks perceived as admission advantages regardless of value-added) or escape from increasingly disadvantaged general-school environments?
content.reduction_path: Qualitative interviews with families of track-admitted and non-admitted students on decision drivers; decomposition of track value-added into signaling (wage/admission returns) versus skill (test-score) components; comparison of track applicants' prior achievement versus admitted students to estimate selectivity tightness over time.
